docs.title: Welcome to Boris AI Documentation
docs.text:
Project in Development

This is a test page for Boris AI documentation using MkDocs and GitHub Pages.

docs.title: 🚀 What is Boris AI?
docs.text: Boris AI is an advanced artificial intelligence system designed to help with various natural language processing and data analysis tasks.

docs.title: 📋 Key Features
docs.text:
• Natural Language Processing: Understanding and generation of text in multiple languages
  • Data Analysis: Advanced tools for analysis and visualization
  • RESTful API: Easy integration with existing applications
  • Machine Learning: Customizable models for different use cases

docs.title: 🛠️ Quick Start
docs.text:
First Steps

If this is your first time using Boris AI, we recommend starting with our Installation Guide.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Java 21 or higher
  • Git
  • Web browser for documentation access

docs.title: Basic Setup
docs.text: ```bash

docs.title: Clone the repository
docs.text: git clone https://github.com/librixsoft/boris-docs.git cd boris-docs

docs.title: Install documentation dependencies
docs.text: ./venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Start local documentation server
docs.text: ./venv/bin/mkdocs serve ```

docs.title: 📚 Documentation
docs.text:
Explore Documentation

  • Installation Guide - How to install Boris AI
  • Configuration - Set up your environment
  • API Reference - Complete API documentation
  • Examples - Practical use cases

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Documentación completa de la API de Boris AI.

docs.title: BorisAI
docs.text: python class BorisAI: def __init__(self, config=None): """Inicializa el cliente de Boris AI""" pass

docs.title: Parámetros
docs.text: • config (dict, opcional): Configuración personalizada

docs.title: generate()
docs.text:
Genera texto basado en un prompt.

python def generate(self, prompt, max_tokens=2048, temperature=0.7): """Genera texto""" pass

Parámetros: - prompt (str): Texto de entrada - max_tokens (int): Máximo número de tokens a generar - temperature (float): Creatividad del modelo (0.0-1.0)

Retorna: GenerateResponse

docs.title: analyze_sentiment()
docs.text:
Analiza el sentimiento de un texto.

python def analyze_sentiment(self, text): """Analiza sentimiento""" pass

Parámetros: - text (str): Texto a analizar

Retorna: SentimentResponse

docs.title: classify()
docs.text:
Clasifica texto en categorías.

python def classify(self, text, categories): """Clasifica texto""" pass

Parámetros: - text (str): Texto a clasificar - categories (list): Lista de categorías posibles

Retorna: ClassificationResponse

docs.title: translate()
docs.text:
Traduce texto entre idiomas.

python def translate(self, text, source_lang, target_lang): """Traduce texto""" pass

Parámetros: - text (str): Texto a traducir - source_lang (str): Código del idioma origen - target_lang (str): Código del idioma destino

Retorna: TranslationResponse

docs.title: GenerateResponse
docs.text: python class GenerateResponse: def __init__(self, text, tokens_used, model): self.text = text self.tokens_used = tokens_used self.model = model

docs.title: SentimentResponse
docs.text: python class SentimentResponse: def __init__(self, label, score, confidence): self.label = label # 'positive', 'negative', 'neutral' self.score = score # -1.0 a 1.0 self.confidence = confidence # 0.0 a 1.0

docs.title: ClassificationResponse
docs.text: python class ClassificationResponse: def __init__(self, category, confidence, scores): self.category = category self.confidence = confidence self.scores = scores # Dict con scores por categoría

docs.title: TranslationResponse
docs.text: python class TranslationResponse: def __init__(self, text, source_lang, target_lang, confidence): self.text = text self.source_lang = source_lang self.target_lang = target_lang self.confidence = confidence

docs.title: Manejo de Errores
docs.text:
```python from boris_ai.exceptions import BorisAIError, APIError, ValidationError

try: response = client.generate("Hola mundo") except APIError as e: print(f"Error de API: {e}") except ValidationError as e: print(f"Error de validación: {e}") except BorisAIError as e: print(f"Error general: {e}") ```

docs.title: Límites y Cuotas
docs.text:
• Máximo tokens por request: 4096
  • Requests por minuto: 60
  • Tamaño máximo de texto: 1MB

docs.title: Ejemplos Complejos
docs.text: Ver Ejemplos para casos de uso avanzados.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Practical use cases and advanced examples of Boris AI.

docs.title: Simple Chatbot
docs.text:
```python from boris_ai import BorisAI

class SimpleChatbot: def init(self): self.client = BorisAI() self.context = []

def chat(self, message):
    self.context.append(f"User: {message}")

    prompt = "\n".join(self.context[-5:]) + "\nAssistant:"
    response = self.client.generate(prompt)

    self.context.append(f"Assistant: {response.text}")
    return response.text

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: bot = SimpleChatbot() while True: user_input = input("You: ") if user_input.lower() == 'exit': break response = bot.chat(user_input) print(f"Bot: {response}") ```

docs.title: Sentiment Analysis
docs.text:
```python from boris_ai import BorisAI

def analyze_sentiment(text): client = BorisAI() sentiment = client.analyze_sentiment(text) return { 'text': text, 'sentiment': sentiment.label, 'score': sentiment.score, 'confidence': sentiment.confidence }

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: result = analyze_sentiment("I love this product!") print(f"Sentiment: {result['sentiment']}") print(f"Confidence: {result['confidence']}") ```

docs.title: Text Generation
docs.text:
```python from boris_ai import BorisAI

def generate_text(prompt, max_tokens=100): client = BorisAI() response = client.generate(prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens) return response.text

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: text = generate_text("Write a poem about technology") print(text) ```

docs.title: Simple Classification
docs.text:
```python from boris_ai import BorisAI

def classify_text(text, categories): client = BorisAI() result = client.classify(text, categories) return { 'category': result.category, 'confidence': result.confidence }

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: categories = ['positive', 'negative', 'neutral'] result = classify_text("The service was excellent", categories) print(f"Category: {result['category']}")

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Boris AI is a local model panel that provides a clean, intuitive interface for managing and interacting with local AI models. The application features a modern sidebar navigation with three main sections: Chat, Models, and Settings.

docs.title: Sidebar Navigation
docs.text:
• Boris AI Logo: Icon with application branding
  • Navigation Menu: Three main sections accessible via buttons
  • Chat: Interactive conversation interface
  • Models: Model management and configuration
  • Settings: Application configuration

docs.title: Status Information
docs.text: The sidebar footer displays real-time system status: - GPU Information: GPU name and VRAM when available - GPU Memory: Free GPU memory - RAM GPU Usage: Memory utilization statistics - Connection Status: Online/Offline indicator

docs.title: Welcome Screen
docs.text:
• Personalized greeting when no models are loaded
  • Quick action suggestions for starting conversations
  • Clean, minimal design for focused interaction

docs.title: Chat Features
docs.text:
• Message History: Scrollable conversation display
  • Token Tracking: Real-time display of input/output tokens and context usage
  • Markdown Support: Rich text rendering for AI responses
  • Typing Indicator: Visual feedback during AI responses
  • Message Controls: New conversation, send, and stop functionality

docs.title: Input Controls
docs.text:
• New Chat Button: Start fresh conversations
  • Message Input: Auto-resizing textarea with keyboard shortcuts
  • Send Button: Submit messages (Enter key support)
  • Stop Button: Interrupt ongoing AI responses

docs.title: Phase Candidates Management
docs.text: The interface manages two types of model phases:

docs.title: Planning Phase
docs.text:
• Immediate Loading: Models loaded directly into VRAM
  • Configuration Display: Context size, GPU layers, temperature
  • VRAM Status: Visual indicators for loaded models
  • Eject Function: Unload models from memory

docs.title: Writing Phase
docs.text:
• On-Demand Loading: Models load during execution
  • Candidate Selection: Choose models for writing tasks
  • Configuration Parameters: Same settings as planning phase
  • Memory Management: Efficient VRAM utilization

docs.title: Available Models Section
docs.text:
• Model Cards: Display detected models with metadata
  • Configuration Panel: Advanced parameter settings
  • Context Size (512-32768)
  • Threads (1-32)
  • GPU Layers (0-99)
  • Batch Size (1-2048)
  • Temperature (0.0-2.0)
  • Max Tokens (1-32768)
  • Phase Assignment: Radio buttons for planning/writing selection
  • Load Actions: Context-aware loading buttons

docs.title: Model Status Indicators
docs.text:
• Available: Ready to load
  • Loaded: Currently in VRAM
  • Loading: In-progress status with spinner
  • Configuration: Expanded parameter panels

docs.title: Application Configuration
docs.text:
• Llama Server Path: Path to the llama-server executable
  • Models Directory: Location for model files
  • Workspace Prefix: Default workspace directory
  • Server Version: Automatic version detection

docs.title: Path Management
docs.text:
• Browse Buttons: File system navigation
  • Relative/Absolute Display: Both path formats shown
  • Validation: Path verification and error handling

docs.title: Installation Management
docs.text:
• Download Button: Automatic llama-server installation
  • Status Indicators: Installation progress and completion
  • Version Control: Core version management

docs.title: Responsive Layout
docs.text:
• Adaptive Design: Works across different screen sizes
  • Collapsible Sections: Efficient space utilization
  • Status Indicators: Real-time feedback

docs.title: User Experience
docs.text:
• Loading States: Visual feedback for all operations
  • Error Handling: Clear error messages and recovery options
  • Keyboard Shortcuts: Efficient navigation and interaction
  • Contextual Actions: Relevant buttons based on current state

docs.title: Visual Design
docs.text:
• Modern UI: Clean, professional interface
  • Status Chips: Color-coded information display
  • Progress Indicators: Loading spinners and progress bars
  • Consistent Styling: Unified design language throughout

docs.title: Frontend Technologies
docs.text:
• Vue.js: Reactive component framework
  • CSS Grid/Flexbox: Responsive layout system
  • SVG Icons: Scalable vector graphics
  • Real-time Updates: Live data synchronization

docs.title: Performance Features
docs.text:
• Lazy Loading: On-demand model loading
  • Memory Management: Efficient VRAM usage
  • Background Operations: Non-blocking UI operations
  • Status Monitoring: Continuous system health checks

This interface provides a comprehensive yet user-friendly environment for managing local AI models, with emphasis on performance, usability, and real-time feedback.

docs.title: Referencia
docs.text: Referencia completa de conceptos y configuraciones avanzadas.

docs.title: Tokens
docs.text:
Los tokens son la unidad básica que Boris AI utiliza para procesar texto:

  • 1 token ≈ 4 caracteres en español
  • 100 tokens ≈ 75 palabras
  • Límite por request: 4096 tokens

docs.title: Modelos Disponibles
docs.text: Modelo Descripción Casos de Uso Límites default Modelo balanceado Uso general 2048 tokens advanced Mayor capacidad Tareas complejas 4096 tokens fast Optimizado para velocidad Respuestas rápidas 1024 tokens custom Entrenado personalmente Dominio específico Variable

docs.title: Temperature
docs.text: Controla la creatividad del modelo: - 0.0-0.3: Respuestas predecibles y factuales - 0.4-0.7: Balance entre creatividad y coherencia - 0.8-1.0: Máxima creatividad (menos predecible)

docs.title: Top P
docs.text: Muestreo de núcleo: - 0.1-0.5: Muy conservador - 0.6-0.9: Balanceado - 0.95-1.0: Muy creativo

docs.title: Frequency Penalty
docs.text: Reduce la repetición: - 0.0: Sin penalización - 0.5: Penalización moderada - 1.0: Máxima penalización

docs.title: Archivo de Configuración Completo
docs.text: ```yaml

docs.title: boris-config.yaml
docs.text:
boris_ai: modelo: "advanced" idioma: "es" max_tokens: 2048 temperature: 0.7 top_p: 0.9 frequency_penalty: 0.5

api: host: "api.boris-ai.com" puerto: 443 ssl: true timeout: 30 retry_attempts: 3

cache: enabled: true ttl: 3600 max_size: "100MB"

logging: nivel: "INFO" archivo: "boris.log" formato: "%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s"

monitoring: metrics_enabled: true trace_requests: true performance_tracking: true ```

docs.title: Variables de Entorno
docs.text: Variable Descripción Valor por Defecto BORIS_API_KEY Clave de API None BORIS_MODEL Modelo a usar default BORIS_BASE_URL URL base de API https://api.boris-ai.com BORIS_TIMEOUT Timeout en segundos 30 BORIS_LOG_LEVEL Nivel de log INFO BORIS_CACHE_DIR Directorio de cache ~/.boris/cache

docs.title: Cliente Singleton
docs.text:
```python class BorisAIClient: _instance = None

def __new__(cls):
    if cls._instance is None:
        cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
        cls._instance.client = BorisAI()
    return cls._instance

```

docs.title: Decorador de Cache
docs.text:
```python import functools import hashlib

def cache_response(ttl=3600): def decorator(func): cache = {}

    @functools.wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        key = hashlib.md5(str(args + tuple(kwargs.items())).encode()).hexdigest()

        if key in cache:
            result, timestamp = cache[key]
            if time.time() - timestamp < ttl:
                return result

        result = func(*args, **kwargs)
        cache[key] = (result, time.time())
        return result

    return wrapper
return decorator

```

docs.title: Manejo de Errores
docs.text:
```python from boris_ai.exceptions import BorisAIError import time from functools import wraps

def retry_on_error(max_retries=3, delay=1): def decorator(func): @wraps(func) def wrapper(args, kwargs): for attempt in range(max_retries): try: return func(args, **kwargs) except BorisAIError as e: if attempt == max_retries - 1: raise time.sleep(delay * (2 ** attempt)) return None return wrapper return decorator ```

docs.title: Métricas Disponibles
docs.text:
• Latencia: Tiempo de respuesta
  • Throughput: Requests por segundo
  • Error Rate: Tasa de errores
  • Token Usage: Consumo de tokens
  • Cache Hit Rate: Eficiencia de cache

docs.title: Integración con Prometheus
docs.text: ```python from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram, Gauge

docs.title: Métricas
docs.text:
REQUEST_COUNT = Counter('boris_requests_total', 'Total requests', ['method', 'endpoint']) REQUEST_LATENCY = Histogram('boris_request_duration_seconds', 'Request latency') TOKEN_USAGE = Counter('boris_tokens_used_total', 'Total tokens used') CACHE_HITS = Gauge('boris_cache_hits', 'Cache hit rate')

@REQUEST_LATENCY.time() def make_request(prompt): REQUEST_COUNT.labels(method='generate', endpoint='/generate').inc() # ... lógica de request ```

docs.title: Batch Processing
docs.text: python def batch_generate(prompts, batch_size=10): """Procesa múltiples prompts eficientemente""" results = [] for i in range(0, len(prompts), batch_size): batch = prompts[i:i + batch_size] batch_results = client.batch_process(batch) results.extend(batch_results) return results

docs.title: Streaming
docs.text: python def stream_generate(prompt): """Genera texto en streaming""" for chunk in client.generate_stream(prompt): yield chunk.text # Procesar chunk inmediatamente

docs.title: API Key Management
docs.text:
```python import os from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

def encrypt_api_key(api_key): key = Fernet.generate_key() f = Fernet(key) encrypted_key = f.encrypt(api_key.encode()) return encrypted_key, key

def decrypt_api_key(encrypted_key, key): f = Fernet(key) return f.decrypt(encrypted_key).decode() ```

docs.title: Validación de Entrada
docs.text:
```python import re

def validate_prompt(prompt): """Valida que el prompt sea seguro""" # Longitud máxima if len(prompt) > 10000: raise ValueError("Prompt demasiado largo")

# Contenido malicioso
if re.search(r'<script|javascript:', prompt, re.IGNORECASE):
    raise ValueError("Contenido no permitido")

return True

```

docs.title: Timeouts
docs.text: ```python

docs.title: Aumentar timeout
docs.text: client = BorisAI(timeout=60) ```

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
```python import time import random

def rate_limited_request(prompt): time.sleep(random.uniform(0.1, 0.5)) # Random delay return client.generate(prompt) ```

docs.title: Memory Issues
docs.text: ```python

docs.title: Procesamiento en chunks
docs.text:
def process_large_text(text, chunk_size=1000): results = [] for i in range(0, len(text), chunk_size): chunk = text[i:i + chunk_size] result = client.process(chunk) results.append(result) del chunk # Liberar memoria return results ```

Esta referencia te ayudará a sacar el máximo provecho de Boris AI en tus proyectos.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Configure Boris AI according to your specific needs.

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
Boris AI uses a config.yaml file for main configuration:

```yaml

docs.title: config.yaml
docs.text:
boris_ai: model: "default" language: "en" max_tokens: 2048 temperature: 0.7

api: host: "localhost" port: 8000 ssl: false

logging: level: "INFO" file: "boris.log" ```

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
You can also configure Boris AI using environment variables:

bash export BORIS_AI_MODEL="advanced" export BORIS_API_KEY="your-api-key" export BORIS_LOG_LEVEL="DEBUG"

docs.title: Available Models
docs.text:
• default: Standard model for general use
  • advanced: Model with greater capacity
  • fast: Model optimized for speed
  • custom: Custom trained model

docs.title: Performance Options
docs.text: yaml performance: threads: 4 memory_cache: "2GB" batch_size: 32

docs.title: Configuration Validation
docs.text:
Use the validation command:

bash boris-cli validate-config

Invalid Configuration

If configuration is invalid, Boris AI will show specific errors to correct them.

docs.title: Next Step
docs.text: Once configured, check Getting Started.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Start using Boris AI with these basic examples.

docs.title: Hello World
docs.text:
Your first program with Boris AI:

```python from boris_ai import BorisAI

docs.title: Initialize the client
docs.text: client = BorisAI()

docs.title: Generate text
docs.text: response = client.generate("Hello, how are you?") print(response.text) ```

docs.title: Sentiment Analysis
docs.text:
Analyze the sentiment of a text:

python sentiment = client.analyze_sentiment("I love this product!") print(f"Sentiment: {sentiment.label}") print(f"Confidence: {sentiment.score}")

docs.title: Text Classification
docs.text:
Classify text into predefined categories:

python categories = ["positive", "negative", "neutral"] result = client.classify("The service was excellent", categories) print(f"Category: {result.category}")

docs.title: Translation
docs.text:
Translate text between languages:

python translation = client.translate( text="Hello, how are you?", source_lang="en", target_lang="es" ) print(f"Translation: {translation.text}")

docs.title: Batch Processing
docs.text:
Process multiple texts efficiently:

```python texts = [ "Text 1 to process", "Text 2 to process", "Text 3 to process" ]

results = client.batch_process(texts) for i, result in enumerate(results): print(f"Text {i+1}: {result}") ```

Best Practices

  • Use batch processing for multiple texts
  • Handle exceptions appropriately
  • Cache results when possible

docs.title: Next Step
docs.text: Explore the API Reference to see all available functionalities.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This guide will help you install Boris AI on your system.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
• Java: 21 or higher (OpenJDK or Oracle JDK)
  • Operating System: Windows 10+, macOS 10.14+, Ubuntu 18.04+
  • RAM Memory: Minimum 4GB, recommended 8GB
  • Disk Space: 1GB of free space

docs.title: Documentation Setup
docs.text:
To set up the Boris AI documentation locally:

```bash

docs.title: Clone the repository
docs.text: git clone https://github.com/librixsoft/boris-docs.git cd boris-docs

docs.title: Install documentation dependencies
docs.text: ./venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Start local documentation server
docs.text:
./venv/bin/mkdocs serve ```

Access Documentation

Open your browser and navigate to http://127.0.0.1:8000 to view the documentation locally.

docs.title: Installation Verification
docs.text:
To verify the documentation setup was successful:

```bash

docs.title: Check if MkDocs is working
docs.text: ./venv/bin/mkdocs --version

docs.title: Build the documentation
docs.text:
./venv/bin/mkdocs build ```

Successful Installation

If the documentation builds without errors and you can access it locally, the setup was successful!

docs.title: Error: "Java not found"
docs.text: Make sure you have Java 21+ installed and in your system PATH: bash java -version javac -version

docs.title: Error: "Permission denied"
docs.text: On macOS/Linux, make sure the virtual environment has proper permissions: bash chmod +x ./venv/bin/*

docs.title: Error: "MkDocs not found"
docs.text: Install the dependencies: bash ./venv/bin/pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Next Step
docs.text: Once installed, continue with Configuration.
